# UC-13.4.10 · AI / LLM Model API Key Rotation Compliance and Cutover Verification

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check that the passwords your AI services use get refreshed on time and that the live gateways actually pick up the new ones. When a team only updates a vault record but the app keeps an old key, we flag it so auditors see real protection—not paperwork alone.*

---

## Description

Detects stale or unverifiable rotation of AI vendor and gateway credentials by fusing secret-store audit evidence from Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, and optional GCP Secret Manager with AI gateway last-used logs. Surfaces key age versus policy thresholds per logical_key_id, dual-key cutover gaps where gateways lag secret-store renewals, failed or missing automation fingerprints, and per-business-unit compliance percentages so audits distinguish cosmetic vault updates from runtime adoption—without conflating latency, token cost, accelerator duty cycle, model versioning, gateway throttles, Ollama abuse, MCP misuse, DSDL lifecycle drift, or MLTK statistical drift covered by sibling UCs.

## Value

Risk and platform leaders receive tamper-evident proof that AI credentials rotate on cadence and that inference paths actually consume the renewed material, which shrinks blast radius from leaked keys, satisfies third-party and internal policy reviews, and gives each business unit a defensible compliance score tied to named owners rather than anonymous infrastructure metrics.

## Implementation

Ingest vault:audit, aws:cloudtrail rotation events, Azure Key Vault diagnostics, and ai_gateway:access into named indexes; maintain ai_secret_inventory.csv with logical_key_id, business_unit, rotation_threshold_days, risk_tier; accelerate CIM Change for secret-adjacent objects; schedule compliance_uc_13_4_10_ai_key_rotation_evidence daily plus hourly paging on critical rows; dashboard table of violations, single value for enterprise compliance, bar chart of key_age_days, heatmap by business_unit.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with identity engineering, cloud security operations, and the AI platform team that operates inference gateways for OpenAI, Azure OpenAI Service, Anthropic, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and internal reverse proxies. This use case is intentionally narrower than UC-13.4.1 latency analytics, UC-13.4.2 token cost telemetry, UC-13.4.3 accelerator utilization, UC-13.4.4 model artifact lineage, UC-13.4.5 gateway rate fairness, UC-13.4.6 self-hosted Ollama abuse patterns, UC-13.4.7 MCP broker misuse, UC-13.4.13 sequence-to-sequence DSDL lifecycle evidence, and UC-13.4.15 MLTK statistical drift. The objective here is audit-grade proof that vendor and gateway credentials rotate on schedule, that secret-store automation actually completes, and that runtime consumers picked up the new material instead of silently continuing with stale values.

RBAC: searches reading secret audit indexes, cloud control-plane trails, and AI gateway access logs must use role-based access with justification workflows. Analysts should see hashed fingerprints or stable aliases rather than raw API keys. Break-glass accounts that bypass rotation require explicit tagging in the inventory lookup so they do not poison compliance percentages.

Data contracts: standardize logical_key_id as the join key between HashiCorp Vault paths, Amazon Secrets Manager ARNs, Azure Key Vault secret names, Google Secret Manager resource names, and gateway configuration aliases. Maintain vendor_scope labels such as openai_org, azure_openai, anthropic_workspace, bedrock_iam, vertex_sa, and internal_llm_proxy so dashboards separate regulated lines of business.

Time synchronization: secret-store audit timestamps and gateway last_used metadata must align within a few minutes; chronically skewed collectors produce imaginary cutover gaps. Require NTP discipline on forwarders shipping vault:audit, AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor diagnostics, GCP audit logs, and gateway containers.

Inventory governance: publish ai_secret_inventory.csv from the same configuration database that provisions Kubernetes secrets, sidecars, and Terraform modules. Columns should include logical_key_id, business_unit, owner, vendor_scope, env_alias, rotation_threshold_days, risk_tier, expected_gateway_cluster, and notes for dual-key migrations.

Retention: keep at least thirteen months of rotation evidence where finance and regulated AI governance demand historical replay. Tier hot storage for incident response and cold storage for annual audits.

Risk briefing: perfect compliance in the secret manager with zero gateway last_used movement after rotation usually means cosmetic rotation—teams updated a dormant path while live inference still reads an older mount. This use case highlights that failure mode by correlating store events with gateway authentication success streams.

Differentiation recap: secret-store audit forensics plus gateway cutover verification for AI credentials—not generic MCP tool abuse, not pure model latency, not FinOps token burn charts.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, HashiCorp Vault enterprise audit devices: enable file or socket audit devices that emit JSON lines with request paths, operations, policies, and identities for the KV and dynamic secret engines that back LLM credentials. Land in index=security with sourcetype=vault:audit. Normalize secret_path or request path fields to logical_key_id during ingestion.

Lane B, AWS Organizations CloudTrail organization trail: ensure data events or management events include secretsmanager RotateSecret, PutSecretValue, and failed rotation attempts for Bedrock-related IAM users if keys are materialized outside Secrets Manager. Use the Splunk Add-on for AWS to parse aws:cloudtrail. Map requestParameters.secretId into logical_key_id aliases that match the inventory CSV.

Lane C, Azure Key Vault diagnostic settings: stream Microsoft.KeyVault vault audit categories SecretSet, SecretRotate, and administrative changes into index=security with sourcetype aligned to the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure parser expectations. Map secret names to logical_key_id and stamp subscription and vault URI dimensions for triage.

Lane D, AI gateway last-used telemetry: configure your inference proxy—whether Envoy with ext_authz, a bespoke Python gateway, or a vendor appliance—to emit structured events on successful authentication containing vault_secret_path or api_key_alias, hashed credential fingerprints, model route, and business_unit when available. Land as sourcetype=ai_gateway:access or llm_proxy:json in index=ai_observability. This lane is mandatory to prove cutover, not merely policy timestamps.

Lane E optional, Google Cloud Audit Logs for Secret Manager: when Vertex service accounts rotate, forward SecretManager SecretVersion Add events through the Splunk Add-on for GCP. If you cannot add a fifth multisearch arm due to search budget, blend these events into a summary index keyed by logical_key_id hourly and union upstream—document that compromise in your deployment notes.

CIM alignment: map high-value gateway authentication failures and successes into the Authentication data model where feasible. Map infrastructure changes that alter Vault policies, Kubernetes secret mounts, or gateway ConfigMaps into the Change data model with Change.object or Change.dest set to logical_key_id strings for tstats joins.

props.conf: TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON, strip raw secrets with SEDCMD, and FIELDALIAS logical aliases consistently. Use INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS only when performance testing proves benefit.

Lookup automation: regenerate ai_secret_inventory.csv whenever Terraform applies change secret metadata. Version the CSV in Git with approvals so dev paths never inherit prod thresholds accidentally.

Supplemental depth for platform readiness: run a quarterly tabletop where identity engineering, cloud security, and AI service owners rehearse a ninety-day rotation with deliberate injection of a failed Lambda rotator. Capture who owns communications to model vendors when org keys must be invalidated early. Store tabletop minutes beside the saved search export so regulators see process—not only telemetry.

Document dependency chains: many gateways read Kubernetes secrets projected as files. When nodes cache secrets, evidence of Vault renew may precede file refresh by several minutes. Tune gateway_cutover_gap grace using environment-specific macros rather than a universal constant if your mesh enforces longer propagation.

For multi-region inference, duplicate logical_key_id labels per region only when inventory explicitly allows; otherwise split keys per region to avoid compliance math that double-counts a single secret path.

Train analysts to read evidence_actions multivalue fields: a sudden dominance of PutSecretValue without RotateSecret may indicate manual emergency rotation—valid yet worth correlating with incident tickets.

When Splunk Cloud search auto_pause triggers during month-end reporting, pre-schedule acceleration maintenance windows and raise dispatch limits temporarily so governance dashboards stay complete.

Partner with FinOps: some teams defer rotation to avoid interrupting long GPU jobs. Encode maintenance windows in env_alias notes and filter those rows from executive heatmaps while retaining them in engineering views.

Archive redacted samples of vault:audit JSON and gateway access lines into a secured knowledge base so new hires learn normal renewal shapes versus policy denial storms.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as compliance_uc_13_4_10_ai_key_rotation_evidence with a daily schedule for governance reporting and an hourly variant for operational paging on critical risk_tier rows. Dispatch earliest=-90d@d unless your policy window is shorter. Alert when severity is critical or high after deduplication on logical_key_id and business_unit. Include recommended_action text in notifications so responders know whether to fix automation, reload gateways, or reconcile dual-key windows.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans out Vault audit, AWS CloudTrail Secrets Manager mutations, Azure Key Vault diagnostics, and AI gateway last-used lanes so a silent lane cannot hide a half-finished rotation. The stats stage separates true secret-store rotation timestamps from gateway activity. join with inputlookup ai_secret_inventory.csv attaches business_unit, owner, and rotation_threshold_days for policy math. tstats on the Change data model counts recent infrastructure touches that should accompany rotations. streamstats computes a short moving average of key_age_days per business_unit to smooth single-day ingestion blips. eventstats derives business_unit compliance percentages for heatmaps. case() assigns severity tiers emphasizing combinations of age violations and gateway cutover gaps. The closing table lists analyst-ready columns for export to audit workpapers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.10 AI LLM API key rotation compliance. Tunables: earliest=-90d@d; inventory columns logical_key_id, business_unit, owner, rotation_threshold_days.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=security sourcetype=vault:audit earliest=-90d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="vault_audit"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(secret_path,path,request_path,data_path,"vault_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="hashicorp_vault"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation,action,type,"noop"))))
      | where match(event_action,"(?i)update|write|rotate|renew|kv-patch")
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
    [ search index=security sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-90d@d latest=now (eventName=RotateSecret OR eventName=PutSecretValue)
      | eval lane="aws_cloudtrail"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(requestParameters.secretId,secret_id,"aws_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="aws_secrets_manager"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(eventName)))
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
    [ search index=security (sourcetype=azure:monitor:microsoft.keyvault OR sourcetype=msazure:keyvault:diagnostic) earliest=-90d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="azure_keyvault"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(secret_name,SecretName,properties_secretName,resource_id,"azure_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="azure_key_vault"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operationName,OperationName,ActivityStatus,"unset"))))
      | where match(event_action,"(?i)secretset|secretrotate|rotate|npr")
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
    [ search index=ai_observability (sourcetype=ai_gateway:access OR sourcetype=llm_proxy:json) earliest=-90d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="ai_gateway_lastused"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(vault_secret_path,api_key_alias,logical_secret_key,k8s_secret_ref,"gateway_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="ai_gateway_projection"
      | eval event_action="gateway_last_used"
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
| eval logical_key_id=if(len(logical_key_id)<3 OR match(logical_key_id,"_unscoped$"),null(),logical_key_id)
| stats
    max(eval(if(lane!="ai_gateway_lastused",last_rotation_hint,null()))) AS last_rotation_ts
    max(eval(if(lane=="ai_gateway_lastused",last_rotation_hint,null()))) AS gateway_last_used
    values(secret_store) AS secret_store_mv
    values(lane) AS lanes
    values(event_action) AS evidence_actions
    by logical_key_id
| eval secret_store=mvindex(secret_store_mv,0)
| join type=left max=0 logical_key_id
    [| inputlookup ai_secret_inventory.csv
     | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(logical_key_id)))
     | eval rotation_threshold_days=tonumber(coalesce(rotation_threshold_days,policy_max_age_days,90))
     | fields logical_key_id business_unit owner vendor_scope env_alias rotation_threshold_days risk_tier ]
| eval rotation_threshold_days=coalesce(rotation_threshold_days,90)
| eval key_age_days=if(isnotnull(last_rotation_ts),round((now()-last_rotation_ts)/86400,2),null())
| eval age_violation=if(isnull(last_rotation_ts) OR key_age_days>rotation_threshold_days,1,0)
| eval gateway_cutover_gap=if(isnotnull(last_rotation_ts) AND isnotnull(gateway_last_used) AND gateway_last_used+300<last_rotation_ts,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 logical_key_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS change_recent FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-30d@d latest=now BY Change.object
     | rename Change.object AS logical_key_id
     | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(logical_key_id))) ]
| eval change_recent=coalesce(change_recent,0)
| sort business_unit + logical_key_id
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f avg(key_age_days) AS ma_age by business_unit
| eventstats mean(key_age_days) AS bu_mean_age sum(age_violation) AS bu_viol dc(logical_key_id) AS bu_keys by business_unit
| eval bu_compliance_pct=round(100*(bu_keys-bu_viol)/nullif(bu_keys,0),2)
| eval severity=case(
    age_violation==1 AND match(lower(coalesce(risk_tier,"")),"crit"),"critical",
    age_violation==1 AND gateway_cutover_gap==1,"critical",
    age_violation==1,"high",
    gateway_cutover_gap==1,"medium",
    ma_age>rotation_threshold_days AND age_violation==0,"medium",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical","halt_inference_rotate_credentials_redeploy_gateways_verify_audit_trail",
    severity=="high","open_sev1_rotation_queue_remediate_stale_materialized_secrets",
    severity=="medium","reconcile_dual_key_cutover_validate_sidecar_hot_reload",
    true(),"record_passing_control_in_governance_repository")
| table logical_key_id secret_store vendor_scope business_unit owner env_alias rotation_threshold_days key_age_days ma_age last_rotation_ts gateway_last_used gateway_cutover_gap change_recent bu_compliance_pct severity recommended_action evidence_actions lanes
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[compliance_uc_13_4_10_ai_key_rotation_evidence_alert]
cron_schedule = 0 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -90d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch compliance_uc_13_4_10_ai_key_rotation_evidence | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if multisearch queue time grows, materialize per-lane summaries into an hourly summary index keyed on logical_key_id and join this alert to summaries for paging while retaining the full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Compare Splunk last_rotation_ts for a canary secret against the Vault UI version history, AWS Secrets Manager rotation history, or Azure portal secret version list for the same hour window. Mismatches usually indicate wrong field extraction or duplicate logical_key_id labels.

Pick three active inference services and verify gateway_last_used advances within fifteen minutes after a planned rotation. If the store shows success but gateway timestamps freeze, inspect sidecar file mounts, stale Kubernetes secrets, and environment variables baked into container images.

Validate business_unit compliance percentages by hand-counting ten inventory rows against spreadsheet expectations. eventstats math errors often trace to null business_unit values—enforce a default like unassigned_bu in the lookup.

Exercise negative control: pause test traffic, confirm info severity dominates, then restore load.

Confirm tstats Change counts align with recent Terraform or Helm changes logged in your change ticketing system; zero change_recent during a rotation may be normal for pure API key swaps or may indicate missing CIM mapping.

Replay a historical dual-key migration week: severity should show medium gateway_cutover_gap rows during the overlap window, then return to info after reload.

Coordinate with internal audit to export the closing table to CSV with signatures from the Head of Platform for quarterly governance packs.

Deep validation passes: correlate a single logical_key_id across Vault entity IDs, AWS principal ARNs, and gateway api_key_alias fields to prove the join key is stable. Run Job Inspector to confirm subsearch cardinality stays within platform limits; if inputlookup grows beyond a few thousand rows, migrate to KVStore collections with automatic pruning.

Spot-check ma_age smoothing: artificially drop one ingestion hour and confirm streamstats does not permanently suppress high severity when the next hour restores data.

For Azure diagnostics, validate OperationName filters against a lab SecretSet event so you do not exclude vendor-specific strings your organization actually emits.

Gateway validation: ensure ai_gateway:access includes a stable tenant dimension when available so business_unit rollups cannot be spoofed by missing fields.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Stale OpenAI organization API key still within live gateway mounts: treat as immediate high severity; rotate at the vendor console, update Vault KV, and force gateway hot reload before accepting closure.

Case B — Amazon Bedrock IAM user access key exceeding enterprise ninety-day standard without Secrets Manager rotation evidence: open cloud security ticket, enable automatic rotation or enforced redeployment, and verify CloudTrail RotateSecret coverage.

Case C — HashiCorp Vault rotation job stuck in CrashLoopBackoff without matching audit success: inspect Kubernetes operator logs, unseal status, and policy paths; silent operators create false compliance if only Lambda checks run.

Case D — Azure Key Vault soft-delete event on a production secret name during business hours: validate change tickets, initiate recovery workflow, and pause dependent inference routes until versions reconcile.

Case E — Dual-key cutover window closed but consumers still authenticate with retired alias: flush Envoy clusters, restart sidecars, and diff live ConfigMaps against inventory expected_gateway_cluster labels.

Case F — Hardcoded API key discovered in container image layers while Vault-managed peer exists: rebuild image without literals, scan CI templates, and mark image digest non-compliant until rebuilt.

Case G — Development logical_key_id promoted to production env_alias without threshold downgrade: fix inventory routing, split keys per environment, and recalculate compliance excluding lab paths.

Case H — Google Vertex service account JSON rotation logged in GCP audit but gateway_last_used references old k8s_secret_ref: patch workload identity bindings and rollout inference pods.

Case I — Anthropic workspace key rotated yet proxy cache serves previous bearer for five minutes: tune cache TTL below rotation SLO or disable auth caching for critical models.

Case J — Leftover canary secret version still referenced by batch scoring job: align job vault paths with primary rotation target and drain queues.

Case K — AWS Secrets Manager rotation Lambda failure without pager while manual PutSecretValue acts as backstop: wire CloudWatch alarms into Splunk and require paired audit events.

Case L — Break-glass static key issued during outage never returned to managed rotation: expire the exception date in ai_secret_inventory.csv notes and chase owner attestation.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists vault_audit, aws_cloudtrail, azure_keyvault, and ai_gateway_lastused arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; closing table includes eighteen analyst-facing columns for audit export.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.10 AI LLM API key rotation compliance. Tunables: earliest=-90d@d; inventory columns logical_key_id, business_unit, owner, rotation_threshold_days.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=security sourcetype=vault:audit earliest=-90d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="vault_audit"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(secret_path,path,request_path,data_path,"vault_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="hashicorp_vault"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation,action,type,"noop"))))
      | where match(event_action,"(?i)update|write|rotate|renew|kv-patch")
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
    [ search index=security sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-90d@d latest=now (eventName=RotateSecret OR eventName=PutSecretValue)
      | eval lane="aws_cloudtrail"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(requestParameters.secretId,secret_id,"aws_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="aws_secrets_manager"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(eventName)))
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
    [ search index=security (sourcetype=azure:monitor:microsoft.keyvault OR sourcetype=msazure:keyvault:diagnostic) earliest=-90d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="azure_keyvault"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(secret_name,SecretName,properties_secretName,resource_id,"azure_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="azure_key_vault"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operationName,OperationName,ActivityStatus,"unset"))))
      | where match(event_action,"(?i)secretset|secretrotate|rotate|npr")
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
    [ search index=ai_observability (sourcetype=ai_gateway:access OR sourcetype=llm_proxy:json) earliest=-90d@d latest=now
      | eval lane="ai_gateway_lastused"
      | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(vault_secret_path,api_key_alias,logical_secret_key,k8s_secret_ref,"gateway_unscoped"))))
      | eval secret_store="ai_gateway_projection"
      | eval event_action="gateway_last_used"
      | eval last_rotation_hint=_time
      | fields _time lane logical_key_id secret_store event_action last_rotation_hint ]
| eval logical_key_id=if(len(logical_key_id)<3 OR match(logical_key_id,"_unscoped$"),null(),logical_key_id)
| stats
    max(eval(if(lane!="ai_gateway_lastused",last_rotation_hint,null()))) AS last_rotation_ts
    max(eval(if(lane=="ai_gateway_lastused",last_rotation_hint,null()))) AS gateway_last_used
    values(secret_store) AS secret_store_mv
    values(lane) AS lanes
    values(event_action) AS evidence_actions
    by logical_key_id
| eval secret_store=mvindex(secret_store_mv,0)
| join type=left max=0 logical_key_id
    [| inputlookup ai_secret_inventory.csv
     | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(logical_key_id)))
     | eval rotation_threshold_days=tonumber(coalesce(rotation_threshold_days,policy_max_age_days,90))
     | fields logical_key_id business_unit owner vendor_scope env_alias rotation_threshold_days risk_tier ]
| eval rotation_threshold_days=coalesce(rotation_threshold_days,90)
| eval key_age_days=if(isnotnull(last_rotation_ts),round((now()-last_rotation_ts)/86400,2),null())
| eval age_violation=if(isnull(last_rotation_ts) OR key_age_days>rotation_threshold_days,1,0)
| eval gateway_cutover_gap=if(isnotnull(last_rotation_ts) AND isnotnull(gateway_last_used) AND gateway_last_used+300<last_rotation_ts,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 logical_key_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS change_recent FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-30d@d latest=now BY Change.object
     | rename Change.object AS logical_key_id
     | eval logical_key_id=lower(trim(toString(logical_key_id))) ]
| eval change_recent=coalesce(change_recent,0)
| sort business_unit + logical_key_id
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f avg(key_age_days) AS ma_age by business_unit
| eventstats mean(key_age_days) AS bu_mean_age sum(age_violation) AS bu_viol dc(logical_key_id) AS bu_keys by business_unit
| eval bu_compliance_pct=round(100*(bu_keys-bu_viol)/nullif(bu_keys,0),2)
| eval severity=case(
    age_violation==1 AND match(lower(coalesce(risk_tier,"")),"crit"),"critical",
    age_violation==1 AND gateway_cutover_gap==1,"critical",
    age_violation==1,"high",
    gateway_cutover_gap==1,"medium",
    ma_age>rotation_threshold_days AND age_violation==0,"medium",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical","halt_inference_rotate_credentials_redeploy_gateways_verify_audit_trail",
    severity=="high","open_sev1_rotation_queue_remediate_stale_materialized_secrets",
    severity=="medium","reconcile_dual_key_cutover_validate_sidecar_hot_reload",
    true(),"record_passing_control_in_governance_repository")
| table logical_key_id secret_store vendor_scope business_unit owner env_alias rotation_threshold_days key_age_days ma_age last_rotation_ts gateway_last_used gateway_cutover_gap change_recent bu_compliance_pct severity recommended_action evidence_actions lanes
```

## Visualization

Primary table of logical_key_id rows with severity and recommended_action; single value panel for enterprise-wide bu_compliance_pct; bar chart of key_age_days distribution by vendor_scope; heatmap of business_unit versus compliance percentage with drilldown to gateway_cutover_gap.

## Known False Positives

Lab environments that share logical_key_id strings with production inflate gateway_last_used and can mask cutover gaps until inventory splits environments. Long-running batch jobs may legitimately delay gateway timestamp movement for hours; correlate job schedules before paging. Azure diagnostic OperationName strings vary by subscription policy; an overly tight match filter drops real SecretSet events and creates false stale-key readings. Vault audit noise from health checks and policy listing can satisfy coarse filters; tighten operation lists once you observe false renew signals. Manual emergency PutSecretValue rotations without RotateSecret still secure the credential but may look unusual in evidence_actions until you annotate the incident ticket. Clock skew between cloud control plane and on-prem gateways produces artificial gateway_cutover_gap positives; monitor NTP offsets. Break-glass keys tagged in inventory still appear as violations if exception workflows forget to extend rotation_threshold_days; governance must update CSV fields. Summary index lag for GCP Secret Manager optional lane can make Vertex rotations look tardy when only multisearch arms run; reconcile with native cloud consoles. Change.object mapping mismatches yield zero change_recent despite real Helm churn; validate CIM transforms for ConfigMap objects tied to logical_key_id. Business units with only a handful of keys show volatile bu_compliance_pct; use rolling monthly panels for executive reporting.

- [HashiCorp Vault audit device documentation](https://developer.hashicorp.com/vault/docs/audit)
- [AWS Secrets Manager rotation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/rotate-secrets.html)
- [Azure Key Vault secret rotation guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/secrets/tutorial-rotation)
- [Google Cloud Secret Manager overview](https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/overview)
- [OWASP Secrets Management Cheat Sheet](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Secrets_Management_Cheat_Sheet.html)
- [Splunk Lantern — getting data in](https://lantern.splunk.com/Splunk_Platform/Data_Management/Getting_Data_In)
- [NIST SP 800-57 cryptographic key management](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-57-part-1/rev-5/final)
- [Splunk Docs — multisearch command](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/SearchReference/Multisearch)
